source.sourcePath: raw/sources/RA/04_제품별 인허가 진행 문서/02_ADD (HAD1417MCW)/▶ FDA/■ 1차 ADD_510(k)_submission_Final_201023.zip/WORD/VOL_007_Appendix F Safety Test Report/005_Attachment 2 National deviations.pdf

sourceSummary.oneLine: Attachment 2 to test report 397950 records Canada, Japan, Korea, and US national differences for IEC 60601-1 medical electrical equipment safety requirements.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports regulatory or conformity review by identifying applicable national deviations and test report verdicts for IEC 60601-1 safety requirements.
keyPoints: Canadian national differences are according to CAN/CSA-C22.2 No. 60601-1:14.
Japan national differences are according to JIS T 0601-1:2017.
Republic of Korea national differences are according to Notification of MFDS No. 2020-12, Annex 1.
US national differences are according to AAMI/IEC 60601-1:2005 + AMD 1:2012.
The power supply box is stated as rated 100-240 V, with Korea also noting 50/60 Hz.

evidence.claim: The document is Attachment 2 National Deviations to test report 397950.
The attachment addresses IEC 60601-1:2005 with corrigenda and amendment 1:2012.
evidence.evidenceText: ATTACHMENT TO TEST REPORT IEC 60601-1:2005, COR1:2006, COR2:2007, AMD:1:2012.

evidence.claim: Canadian differences are according to CAN/CSA-C22.2 No. 60601-1:14.
Canada calls IEC Canadian national differences Canadian deviations.
evidence.evidenceText: Note *: IEC CANADIAN NATIONAL DIFFERENCES in Canada are called CANADIAN DEVIATIONS.

evidence.claim: Japan differences are according to JIS T 0601-1:2017.
evidence.evidenceText: Differences according to ............... : National standard JIS T 0601-1:2017 (IEC 60601-1:2005 + A1:2012(MOD))

evidence.claim: Korea differences are according to Notification of MFDS No. 2020-12, Annex 1.
evidence.evidenceText: Differences according to ............... : Notification of MFDS No. 2020-12, Annex 1

evidence.claim: Korean national supply voltages are 110 V, 220 V, and 380 V.
evidence.evidenceText: Voltage National supply voltages are 110, 220 V and 380 V.
evidence.pageTarget: Korea medical electrical equipment supply voltage requirements

evidence.claim: Korea accepts appliances having supply frequency of 60 Hz or a frequency range including 60 Hz.
evidence.evidenceText: Frequency Only appliances having supply frequency of 60 Hz or a frequency range including 60 Hz are accepted.
evidence.pageTarget: Korea medical electrical equipment supply frequency requirements

evidence.claim: Korean safety-related instruction manuals and markings shall be in Korean or graphical symbols under IEC Publication 417.
evidence.evidenceText: Instruction manuals and appliance markings related safety, including nameplate shall be in Korean or graphical symbols in accordance with IEC Publication 417.
evidence.pageTarget: Korean medical device safety labeling language requirements

evidence.claim: Korean plugs for supply mains connection shall comply with KSC 8305 and 8300.
evidence.evidenceText: Plug Plugs for connection of the equipment to the supply mains shall comply with the Korean Standard (KSC 8305 and 8300)
evidence.pageTarget: Korean mains plug requirements for medical electrical equipment

evidence.claim: US differences are according to AAMI/IEC 60601-1:2005 + AMD 1:2012.
evidence.evidenceText: Differences according to ............... : National standard AAMI/IEC 60601-1:2005 + AMD 1:2012

evidence.claim: US earth leakage current allowable values are 5 mA in normal condition and 10 mA in single fault condition.
evidence.evidenceText: The allowable values of the EARTH LEAKAGE CURRENT are 5 mA in NORMAL CONDITION and 10 mA in SINGLE FAULT CONDITION
evidence.pageTarget: US IEC 60601-1 earth leakage current requirements

evidence.claim: The power supply box is rated 100-240 V.
evidence.evidenceText: The power supply box is rated as 100-240 V.

evidence.claim: The power supply box is rated 50/60 Hz for Korea frequency evaluation.
evidence.evidenceText: The power supply box is rated as 50/60 Hz.

evidence.claim: The Japan power supply cord set was not evaluated.
evidence.evidenceText: Power supply cord set for Japan is not evaluated, refer to the summary of testing in main test report No. 397950.
evidence.pageTarget: Report 397950 Japan power supply cord evaluation

evidence.claim: The Korea power supply cord set was not evaluated.
evidence.evidenceText: Power supply cord set for Korea is not evaluated, refer to the summary of testing in main test report No. 397950.
evidence.pageTarget: Report 397950 Korea power supply cord evaluation

reviewFlags.reason: No product model is stated in the source text.
No overall document date is stated; only master attachment dates are present for individual national-difference attachments.
Authority signal metadata says FDA, but source text covers Canada, Japan, Korea, and US national differences rather than an FDA-authored document.
Several evaluations refer to main test report No. 397950 and attachments not included in the provided source text.
